subject: Physics
title: The Twin Paradox
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Twin Paradox.

hook.text: Send one twin on a near-light-speed trip. When she returns, she's younger than her sibling.

buildup.text: Time dilation means the traveling twin's clock runs slower. She ages less during the journey.

discovery.text: If each twin sees the other moving, shouldn't both age slower? That's the seeming paradox.

twist.text: The traveling twin accelerates and decelerates. That breaks the symmetry—only she ages less.

climax.text: The paradox is resolved by general relativity. Acceleration makes the difference real and measurable.

punchline.text: One twin travels. One stays. They meet—different ages.

quiz.question: Why does the traveling twin age less in the twin paradox?
options: She accelerates and decelerates, breaking the symmetry
She moves in a straight line at constant speed
Gravity on Earth ages the other twin faster
